eastern_siberia_upper_paleolithic_sunghi.symbolism: Material, map, or monument imagery aligned with the teaching extent; see description and primary sources for detail.

atlas_mountains_iberomaurusian_iberic.symbolism: Backed blades, ostrich-eggshell beads, and open-air art in arid refugia: Maghrebian technocomplexes tie mobility to water and view-sheds.

northeast_africa_aterian_msa.symbolism: Material, map, or monument imagery aligned with the teaching extent; see description and primary sources for detail.

southern_africa_later_stone_age_micro.symbolism: Howiesons Poort, Still Bay, and later microliths; engraved ochre, shell jewellery, and coastal adaptation as negotiable ‘modern’ signals.

doggerland_paleocoast_pleistocene.symbolism: Material, map, or monument imagery aligned with the teaching extent; see description and primary sources for detail.

zanzibar_kenya_rift_epipaleolithic.symbolism: Material, map, or monument imagery aligned with the teaching extent; see description and primary sources for detail.

ppn_b_levant_anatolia_west.symbolism: Material, map, or monument imagery aligned with the teaching extent; see description and primary sources for detail.

halaf_mesopotamia_northern.symbolism: Material, map, or monument imagery aligned with the teaching extent; see description and primary sources for detail.

uruk_sumer_urban_heartland.symbolism: Sealed clay envelopes, bevel-rim bowls, and temple-led redistribution: early cuneiform is accounting theatre before belles lettres.

viking_scandinavia.symbolism: Oseberg textiles, stave churches, and rune-stone public memory: coastal prestige loops tie fjords to the Dnieper and Volga.

epipaleolithic_zagros.symbolism: Caves and highland forager gardens lead toward Neolithic pack animals in the Fertile Crescent’s eastern arc—local sequences, not one wave.

lgm_refugium_sw_europe.imageCaption: Western Europe in the LGM, schematic (Commons).
lgm_refugium_sw_europe.symbolism: Mammoth-steppe, river corridors, and refugial art provinces: recolonisation routes structure later Gravettian and Magdalenian geographies.

mehrgarh_precursor.symbolism: Cereal harvesting, bucrania, and long-distance bead routes prefigure Indus chalcolithic—highland border of the 'Greater Indus' world.

yamnaya_pontic_caspian_herding.symbolism: Cattle, wagons, and kurgan stelae broadcast mobile pastoral charisma; male-focused grave kits seed later steppe ideologies in archaeological debate.

old_kingdom_egypt.symbolism: Colossal cut stone, royal tomb texts, and solar cult: pyramids as state theology cast in engineering.

hellenistic_world.symbolism: Gigantomachy reliefs, ruler cult, and public libraries: kingship framed as saved cosmology in marble.

roman_empire_117.symbolism: Spiral frieze, triumphal ludi, and civic basilicas: law and war narrated in stone around the capitol’s ritual calendar.

gupta_india.symbolism: Buddha mandalas, Sanskrit court drama, and temple maths: a classical kosmos painted and patronised under rajas and sanghas.

british_empire_max.symbolism: Crimson atlases, exhibitionary machinery, and oceanic telegraph: empire rendered as a navigable, colour-coded system.

mississippian.symbolism: Earthwork capitals, moon-calibrated maize, and copper-covered elites: a riverine world without Eurasian draft animals.

mississippian_cahokia.symbolism: Civic-ceremonial core of the Mississippian world: mounds, plazas, and ritually charged shell/copper that encode ranked alliance.

sahul_pleistocene_holocene.symbolism: Lakeshore cremations, megafauna overkill debates, and arid-land refugia: the continent’s first millennia as contested science.

clovis_plano_sphere.symbolism: Fluted points, kill sites, and megafaunal extinctions: Paleoindian time depth without Old World neolithic sheen.

parthian_empire_iran_mesopotamia.symbolism: Equestrian lords, rock reliefs, and a silk-road pluralism: Iranian kingship in dialogue with Hellenic façades and Asian trade cities.

kushan_empire_silk_road.symbolism: Buddha coinage, Greco-Roman atelier styles, and Gandharan stūpa: steppe cavalry + Indian urbanisation as visible dharma display.

scythian_pontic_steppe_horizon.symbolism: Kurgan burials, animal-style metalwork, and long-range relay exchange: Ponto-Caspian prestige without a single imperial capital.

hopewell_earthwork_ohio_valley.symbolism: Riverside earthworks, mica and copper from afar, and long-distance ‘interaction’ without Mesoamerican state forms.

nok_terraced_plateau_nigeria.symbolism: Lifesize heads, iron smelting debate, and early West African figuration—symbolic power before the Sahel’s later empires.

ban_chiang_prehistoric_thailand.symbolism: Painted pottery, early metallurgy, and mortuary rites: a mainland Southeast Asian narrative distinct from the Yellow River only.

maritime_se_asia_srivijaya_era.symbolism: Buddha mandala architecture, strait emporia, and Sanskrit titles on Sumatra and Java: thalassocracy with temple receipts.

lanna_temple_states_golden_mandala.imageCaption: Wat Phra That Doi Suthep, Chiang Mai (Commons).
lanna_temple_states_golden_mandala.symbolism: Chedi gold umbrellas, dhamma-sāsana inscriptions, and highland vassalage: a Tai-Buddhist visual order alongside Burmese and Khmer worlds.

swahili_coast_stone_towns.symbolism: Coral rag mosques, merchant chronicles, and monsoon scripts: the coast as a bilingual Persian–Arabic–Bantu public sphere.

celtic_oppida_west_central_europe.symbolism: Hill-fort renaissance, late La Tène art, and Roman-facing diplomacy: ‘Celtic’ as urbanising Iron Age, not a romantic stereotype only.

seleucid_hellenistic_west_asia.symbolism: Basilikoi, refounded poleis, and Iranian satrapies under Greek royal titulature: a patchwork of cities and camps, not a Roman census map.

sasanian_empire_iran_iraq.symbolism: Ātashkadeh courts, Shapurian rock investitures, and Middle Persian epigraphy: Iranshahr as cosmo-religious theatre on Rome’s eastern rim.

neo_assyrian_heartland.symbolism: Lamassu, deportation inscriptions, and state workshops: terror-and-gift as legible power along the Tigris corridor.

moche_north_peru_ceramic.imageCaption: Moche portrait vessel (Commons / Art Institute of Chicago).
moche_north_peru_ceramic.symbolism: Stirrup-spout pots, regalia in clay, and Andean water cult debate: a coastal theocracy in ceramic, not a single imperial grid.

nazca_south_coast_culture.symbolism: Pampa geoglyphs, qanat-linked ritual fields, and Paracas forebears: arid-surface processions and water politics as public geometry.

adena_mound_ohio_valley.symbolism: Conical mounds, copper celts, and log-tomb inhumations: Ohio Valley mortuary bling before the full Hopewell exchange surge.

hunnic_eurasian_steppe_era.imageCaption: Roman and Hunnic zones, c. 450, schematic (Commons).
hunnic_eurasian_steppe_era.symbolism: Saddle diplomacy, gold diadems, and Roman subventions: steppe confederation politics, not a stable taxation boundary.

phoenician_levantine_maritime.symbolism: Tyrian purple, oared commerce, and pilot alphabets: a city-state lattice of harbours, not a unified inland empire.

bai_nanzhao_southwest_china.symbolism: Buddha-bridge stūpa rows, Dali inscriptions, and Yunnan–Tibetan–Chinese overlap: a highland Tai-Bai corridor, not a Han prefecture read.

east_polynesian_voyaging_horizon.symbolism: Ahu platforms, star-compass seafaring, and tapu lineage: East Polynesian politics as re-established sacred geography, not a European island chart.
